headline: Energy Stocks Surged 38% in Q1 While the Rest of the Market Fell
summary: Energy surged nearly 38% in Q1 2026 as the S&P 500 fell, with upstream producers averaging 45% gains and the Big Three refiners returning nearly 49% on tight fundamentals.

headline: Citi Raises Exxon Mobil (XOM) Price Target on Middle East-Driven Energy Repricing
summary: Exxon Mobil Corporation’s (NYSE:XOM) is included among the 15 Best Consistent Dividend Stocks to Buy Right Now. On April 2, Citi analyst Alastair Syme raised Exxon Mobil Corporation’s (NYSE:XOM) price recommendation to $175 from $150. It reiterated a Neutral rating on the shares. The firm pointed to the Middle East conflict as a key factor. […]

headline: US Shale Drillers Seen Lifting Crude Output on Hormuz-Driven Price Rally
summary: (Bloomberg) -- US shale drillers are expected to follow President Donald Trump’s call for higher oil production — but not just because he said so. The 72% surge in crude prices since the US and Israel commenced attacks on Iran roughly five weeks ago is incentive enough to compel American oil executives to ramp up output, according to observers as diverse as Citigroup Inc. and Enverus Inc.Shale explorers require oil prices somewhere between $62 and $70 a barrel to turn a profit on new wells, acco

headline: Greenland oil drilling will be 'solution' for US, European energy security
summary: Greenland Energy Company (GLND) went public and began trading on the Nasdaq via an IPO on Tuesday, as the company seeks to start a massive onshore oil drilling operation in Greenland by the end of this year.Greenland Energy CEO Robert Price sits down with Yahoo Finance Breaking News Reporter Jake Conley to talk about the company's drilling timeline and the opportunity this will be present for US and European buyers.

headline: RBC expects these oil majors to gain from LNG price dislocations
summary: Investing.com -- Global LNG exports fell 4% quarter-on-quarter in the first three months of 2026 to 115 million tonnes, primarily due to Qatar’s March shutdown, though RBC Capital Markets said oil majors with large U.S. positions stand to capitalise on widening regional price spreads triggered by the conflict in Iran.

headline: What Exxon Mobil (XOM)'s Helium Edge and New Debt Deal Mean For Shareholders
summary: In late March 2026, Exxon Mobil completed a US$169.312 million offering of unsecured, unsubordinated floating rate notes due 2076, while also featuring its circular products business at the World Chemical Forum 2026 and reportedly exploring a breakup sale of its New Zealand operations. At the same time, Exxon Mobil has been benefiting from global oil and helium supply disruptions, with its LaBarge facility helping address the helium shortage. We’ll now examine how Exxon's strengthened...
